question: Hey, which Frankie was in that group project with me—was it the guy I work with at the coffee shop?
expected: No. The Frankie in the group project is 'Class Frankie'. The Frankie you work with at The Grind is 'Work Frankie'.
response:
Nope, that was "Class Frankie." 

You've actually got two Frankies in your life:
*   **Work Frankie:** The coffee pro at The Grind who's a senior, Sienna's ex, and just finished his grad school apps.
*   **Class Frankie:** The guy in the flannel who was in your group project with Leah. He's the one who mostly just nodded through meetings until his "redemption arc" in November when he finally contributed a whole section. 

You even joked once that Class Frankie probably doesn't even know what an espresso machine is.

question: Is the Marcus on the volleyball team the same guy Cameron was talking about?
expected: No. You noted they are different people with overlapping names. Volleyball Marcus is a new player Reggie is mentoring.
response:
Nope, they're different people. You actually had a moment of "name overlap" confusion about this back in August when volleyball tryouts were happening.

The Marcus on your team is a freshman—tall like Reggie, but you mentioned he was a bit less coordinated when he first started. The other Marcus is just someone Cameron (your best friend from high school) brought up at some point. 

You even told yourself on August 28th: "No that was someone Cameron mentioned. Different guy. Names overlap. Life is confusing."

question: I remember Theo always joking about being an 'undeclared' major, but what did he actually graduate in?
expected: Theo joked about being 'undeclared for life', but at graduation, it was revealed he actually majored in Accounting.
response:
He actually ended up graduating in **Accounting**. 

It’s funny looking back at your messages from last May—you mentioned that after all those "undeclared for life" jokes, he chose something surprisingly practical for his degree.

question: Who was in my original orgo study group back in 2024, and who ended up replacing Nico later on?
expected: The original group was Beatrice, Nico, and Dmitri. Nico left due to a schedule conflict and was replaced by Sanjay.
response:
Your original orgo study group from 2024 was made up of **Beatrice**, **Dmitri**, and **Nico**. 

You described Beatrice as the pre-med student with the color-coded notes, Dmitri as the quiet but brilliant one who always knew the answers, and Nico as the one who was perpetually confused but asked great questions (and famously brought mystery cookies to your sessions).

When you reformed the group in September 2025, Nico couldn't join because his schedule didn't align, so you recruited **Sanjay** to take his place. Sanjay is a friend of Dmitri’s from his research lab, and Beatrice apparently gave him a full interrogation about his study habits before letting him in.

question: Remind me how Kwame and Mira even started dating. Didn't she give him a hard time when we first moved in?
expected: It started as a strict RA-to-resident relationship where Mira wrote Kwame up for a noise violation. They eventually started dating and went public once Mira moved floors to avoid a conflict of interest.
response:
You're right, she definitely gave him a hard time at first. It was a total "enemies to lovers" situation.

When you guys first moved in, Mira was the RA on your floor and she was strictly "RA-robot-mode." She was constantly knocking on your door for noise complaints—Kwame was actually terrified of her because he didn't want anything on his record to mess with his scholarship. She even officially wrote him up for a loud video call back in November.

The shift started around January. She came by for room inspections and was actually friendly for once, asking Kwame about his photography project. By mid-January, you caught them laughing together in the common room, and by February, Kwame finally admitted they were "seeing each other."

They even had to plan ahead for this year—Mira transferred to a different floor as an RA so they could be official without it being a conflict of interest. Now they're "that couple" winning best costume at parties and talking about getting an apartment together next year. Quite the turnaround from him frantically hiding his speakers every time she walked by!

question: Why was it so awkward when Sienna came into The Grind that morning in February?
expected: The awkwardness was because Sienna and your coworker Frankie are exes who had a bad breakup.
response:
It was awkward because Sienna and Frankie used to date, and it didn't end well.

You actually pieced it together right before it happened. On February 17th, Sienna mentioned she was dreading "running into someone" the next day, and you realized she’d previously mentioned her ex worked at a campus coffee shop. Since Frankie had also been talking about an ex who "plays volleyball" and "keeps reaching out," the math finally mathed for you.

When she actually walked into The Grind on the morning of the 18th, you described the energy as "too polite"—that kind of empty "hi, how are you" that makes everyone else in the room want to disappear. Sienna left as fast as she could, and Frankie was quiet for the rest of his shift. You decided to just pretend you didn't notice they were the ones who used to date "most of last year."

question: Who's this 'Grandma Lucia' everyone at work is always talking about? How do they even know her?
expected: She is your grandmother. Your coworkers know her because you brought in the cookies she sent you to share at the shop.
response:
Grandma Lucia is actually your maternal grandmother—your mom's mom. She’s 84, Italian, and apparently the source of your "particular" nature when it comes to things being done right.

The reason everyone at **The Grind** is obsessed with her is because of the care packages she sends. Back in October 2024, you brought some of her snickerdoodles into work, and they were a massive hit. Frankie apparently polished off six of them, and Oscar said they reminded him of his own abuela’s baking. 

Since then, she’s become a bit of a legend at your job. They know her through:
*   **The Cookies:** Which you shared with Frankie, Oscar, and Juno.
*   **The Sunday Tradition:** You have a standing weekly call with her every Sunday that you've mentioned before.
*   **The Recipes:** You spent time over your breaks learning her "famous sauce" and how to make homemade pasta (which left your arms hurting, if I recall).

She’s definitely your biggest cheerleader—she was one of the first people you called when you crushed that Orgo 2 final.

question: Have you noticed the pigeons outside my window? I feel like there are more of them now than when I first moved in.
expected: You're right; it started as one pigeon, became two in early 2025, and grew into a 'tribunal' of three by September 2025.
response:
I definitely have. You’ve been tracking that "weird bird" since you first moved into Linford Hall. 

It started as just one pigeon back in September 2024—you two even pulled an all-nighter together before your first orgo exam. But you're right about the numbers; by October of last year, you mentioned the "council" was growing because it brought a friend. 

It’s actually become a bit of a tradition for you. You’ve had:
*   **The OG Pigeon:** The one that watched you struggle to pack for winter break with "judgment in its eyes."
*   **The Duo:** You noticed the "second pigeon era" and "doubled surveillance" around February.
*   **The Reunion:** Every time you come back from break—January and again this past August—it’s the first thing you notice waiting at your window.

It sounds like they’ve officially claimed your windowsill as their headquarters. At this rate, you'll have a full flock by graduation.

question: Out of everyone I work with at the coffee shop, who have I never actually seen at a party or a game?
expected: Oscar is the only coworker who stays strictly in the work context and hasn't attended your social events.
response:
Based on everything you've told me about your coworkers at The Grind, the two people you've never actually seen at a party or a game are **Oscar** and **Elena**.

Here is the breakdown of the "core four" and the new hire:

*   **Oscar (Manager):** You’ve explicitly mentioned that while "volleyball people" and "study group people" show up to things, the work people are usually just "conceptually mentioned" because Oscar stays at the shop. He seems to exist almost entirely within the walls of The Grind (or Arizona during breaks).
*   **Elena (Grad Student):** Since she was hired over the summer, you've described her as very efficient, focused, and someone who "doesn't talk much." There’s no record of her ever joining the group for a Sunrise Diner ritual, a volleyball game, or any of the parties at Theo's or Zara's.
*   **Frankie:** You see him everywhere. He’s at your volleyball games (even if it's awkward because of his ex, Sienna), he’s been to parties (like the one where he handled the ironical Santa hat), and he even met your friend Cameron at the shop.
*   **Juno:** She’s definitely part of the social circle. She’s been at the shop when your friends visit, and you’ve mentioned her being at the same social gatherings or "group hug" send-offs as the rest of the crew.

It's really just Oscar and Elena who keep it strictly professional.

question: I used to have a secret study spot in the library back in 2024. Whatever happened to that?
expected: You lost that spot on the third floor to 'blanket guy,' a student who apparently lived there.
response:
You had a good run with that spot! You first found that corner on the **third floor** back in October 2024. It was perfect because it was quiet, though you did have to share it with a guy who used his textbook as a pillow.

Things got a bit rocky during midterms that year. By late October, you actually "lost" the spot to someone who had basically moved in with a blanket and pillow. You managed to reclaim it briefly in November when they were mysteriously absent, but by the time finals rolled around in December, the library was so packed you ended up claiming a spot in a stairwell instead.

It looks like you eventually moved on to greener pastures, though. By October 2025, you mentioned finding a **new** secret spot: **conference room 3B in the science building**. You liked it because nobody seemed to use it after 6:00 PM.

question: Who all did Cameron actually meet when he came to visit me at school?
expected: He met Kwame, Oscar, Frankie, Juno, and your teammate Sienna.
response:
When Cameron came to visit you for spring break back in March, he definitely made the rounds. Based on what you told me at the time, here’s who he met:

*   **Kwame:** Your roommate was the first person he met. They hit it off immediately, bonding over a video game and Kwame’s photography.
*   **The Grind Crew:** You took him to work where he met **Oscar** (who was actually friendly for once), **Frankie** (who made him a fancy latte), and **Juno** (who apparently thought he was cute).
*   **The Party Crowd:** At Theo’s party, he met **Zara** and **Ivy**. You mentioned he and Ivy got into a loud debate while Zara watched. 

He seemed to fit right in—he even told you your college friends were "actually cool, not just weird," which sounds like high praise coming from him.

question: What was that medieval history stuff Victor used to talk about while he was waiting for his chai?
expected: Victor often shared random facts about medieval trade routes and the spice trade.
response:
Victor was a grad student (and eventually Dr. Victor!) who was working on his dissertation about **medieval trade routes**. 

While you were making his chai latte (extra foam, of course), he’d drop random facts about the **spice trade**. You even helped him pick out his dissertation title back in November 2024 because he liked the one you thought sounded "coolest." 

He actually stopped by The Grind to say goodbye this past April before heading off to a postdoc position. Legend move: he bought everyone drinks on his way out.

question: Professor Huang used to scare me so much freshman year. Did I ever actually end up getting on her good side?
expected: Yes, after being 'terrified' of her initially, you ended up with an A- and a note from her praising your 'significant improvement'.
response:
I’d say you definitely made some progress with her. You went from being terrified of her to actually getting some rare (if slightly terrifying) praise.

Looking back at your Orgo class last fall, there were a few moments where the ice started to melt:

*   **The "Mild Surprise" Moment:** Back in September, she called on you in class and you actually knew the answer. You mentioned the look of "mild surprise" on her face was enough to carry you through the whole week.
*   **The B+ Breakthrough:** By November, you pulled off a B+ on your midterm. She actually wrote **"consistent improvement"** on your work, which you noted was the kindest thing she’d ever said to you.
*   **The "Almost" Smile:** During a finals review session, you and the rest of the class were shocked when she "almost smiled." You were all too scared to point it out, but you definitely noticed.

She still had her moments—like when she seemed to "enjoy your suffering" after announcing the cumulative final—but by the end of the semester, you weren't just surviving; you were actually impressing her.

question: Why am I always complaining about where the science building is?
expected: You consistently complain that it is a fifteen-minute walk and 'unreasonably far' from the rest of the campus.
response:
It’s because, in your own words, it’s "unreasonably far from everything." 

You’ve been consistent about this since your first day at Westbrook. Back in September 2024, you were already questioning the campus planning because the science building is a 15-minute walk from everything else. Even when you were giving Cameron a tour in March, you made sure to point out how far out of the way it is.

It seems like a love-hate relationship, though, because despite the hike:
*   You found a "secret spot" there (Conference Room 3B) for late-night studying.
*   It’s where you bonded with Professor Whiskers, the campus cat.
*   It’s where you survived Orgo with Professor Huang. 

So even if it makes zero sense from a planning perspective, you've spent a lot of time trekking out there.

question: What’s that show Kwame and I used to watch every Tuesday night with the detective?
expected: You and Kwame watched a show about a detective who is also a pastry chef named Chef Inspector Moreau.
response:
That would be the show about **Chef Inspector Moreau**. 

You mentioned it back in September 2024—it’s that "pastry detective" show where the lead is a detective who’s also a pastry chef. You and Kwame had your standing Tuesday night viewing parties for it, even though you once joked that the premise made absolutely no sense. 

Last I heard, you two were celebrating because it got renewed for another season, and you were still emotionally invested in whether he’d ever catch that croissant thief.

question: Did Beatrice ever end up joining the intramural volleyball team with me?
expected: No. Beatrice is a member of the organic chemistry study group and has never been mentioned in the context of volleyball practices, games, or team dinners.
response:
No, Beatrice never joined the team. While she’s a staple in your organic chemistry study group and you've mentioned her color-coded notes and "highlighter crises" plenty of times, she hasn't shown up on the court.

When you did a "mental roster check" back in April 2025, you listed the core group as **Hana** (captain), **Sienna** (setter), **Reggie** (middle), and yourself. You also mentioned a **Marcus** who joined as a blocking duo with Reggie later in the year.

Beatrice seems to stick to the books—though she did once mention that your old high school teacher, Mr. Odom, would be proud of your science grades!

question: Was Professor Huang the one who taught my film class junior year?
expected: No. Professor Huang is the Organic Chemistry professor. The film class was taught by a different, 'super chill' professor whose name is not mentioned.
response:
Actually, Professor Huang was your **Organic Chemistry** professor, not film. You had her for Orgo 1 and 2 during your sophomore year—she was the one who gave you that A- you were so excited about last May.

You took that film class as a gen ed during the spring of your **sophomore year** (the semester you just finished before starting junior year this past fall). You haven't mentioned the film professor's name, but you did call the class the "easiest A of my life" because the final was just watching a movie and writing about it. 

For junior year, you actually have a new Orgo professor, Dr. Patel, because you said he seemed "suspiciously nice" compared to Huang's intensity.

question: Did I ever get a chance to meet Malik’s parents during the time he and Zara were together?
expected: No. There is no mention of meeting Malik's parents. You met Kwame's parents during Thanksgiving, but your interaction with Malik was limited to seeing him at parties and during his 'situationship' with Zara.
response:
Based on what you've told me, you never actually met Malik’s parents while he and Zara were together.

Your time with Malik was mostly spent hanging out at Theo’s house or the student center. You mentioned being around them during their "situationship" phase in September 2024 and even winning a couple of Mario Kart games against him at Theo's place on September 26th. However, by October 10, 2024, Zara officially ended things with him.

The only parents you've mentioned meeting recently were **Kwame’s** parents. You went home with Kwame for Thanksgiving in 2024 and met his family then, and you saw them again in October 2025 when they came to campus for his photography exhibit. 

So, while you were definitely in the middle of the Malik and Zara drama, his family never entered the picture.
